beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, light breakfast, check weather and camera gear.
beats.reasoning: Early riser habit. Warm Friday ahead—Aiden will want to plan around the weather and potential Pride crowds. Brings camera as always.

beats.activity: Drive to Macrina bakery in Kirkland.
beats.reasoning: Across-town commute from Roosevelt to Kirkland, ~30 min drive in light early traffic.

beats.activity: Laminating croissants, pain au chocolat, and managing morning pastry service.
beats.reasoning: Core work shift. Aiden thrives in the meditative rhythm of lamination and early-morning prep before customer rush.

beats.activity: Lunch break in break room, sandwich and tea.
beats.reasoning: Quick meal during shift. Aiden prefers quiet solitude over crowds, so stays at work rather than venturing out.

beats.activity: Afternoon pastry service, closing tasks, and cleanup.
beats.reasoning: Completing shift through end of lunch service and early afternoon. Warm day means steady foot traffic and focus on execution.

beats.activity: Drive back to Roosevelt, navigate around Pride street closures on Broadway and Pike.
beats.reasoning: Kirkland to Roosevelt, ~35–40 min accounting for Friday traffic and potential congestion near Capitol Hill Pride festivities. Aiden avoids crowds, so doesn't venture into the fair itself.

beats.activity: Stop at QFC for fresh groceries and wine, walk home through Roosevelt.
beats.reasoning: Routine Friday errand. Warm, breezy evening is perfect for a neighborhood walk. Aiden values time in his own neighborhood over downtown crowds.

beats.activity: Camera walk through Roosevelt, capturing golden-hour light and street scenes.
beats.reasoning: Warm, breezy Friday with clear light—perfect for photography. Aiden carries his camera everywhere and loves documenting his neighborhood at dusk. Avoids Pride crowds by staying local.

beats.activity: Cook a simple dinner with fresh groceries, review photos on camera.
beats.reasoning: Unwinding at home with a creative meal prep and photo review. Reflects Aiden's preference for intimate solitude over big social events.

diary:
Woke early to catch the light—always do on Fridays. Coffee tasted better than usual, maybe because I knew the day had that Friday energy already baked in. Macrina was steady, the kind of morning where your hands just know what to do. Croissants laminating themselves, almost. The break room sandwich hit different today.

Driving home, I hit the Pride crowds around Broadway and Pike. Street fair already buzzing at 6pm, all that color and movement. Took the long way through Roosevelt instead. Grabbed wine and greens at QFC, then couldn't resist the golden hour—walked around with my camera for an hour, just shooting. The light was *perfect* on the old Victorians, that honeyed angle you wait all week for.

Cooked something simple, reviewed the shots. Found a few keepers. There's something about capturing a neighborhood you walk every day—makes you see it differently. Felt settled.
thought_process: I chose to lean into the camera walk because today felt like permission—the weather was too good to waste, and Fridays always make me restless anyway. The Pride street closures actually worked in my favor; instead of fighting downtown traffic, I stayed in my pocket of Roosevelt and really looked around. Work was solid but background noise; the laminating is muscle memory now. The real decision was whether to join the crowds downtown or stay quiet. I needed quiet today. Picking up the camera and wine felt like the right rhythm—something creative, something to unwind with. My mood shifted from the morning's autopilot routine into something more present once I hit that golden hour light.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
